description: PM alignment coaching skills grounded in what designers and engineers say about their best product managers

skills.name: orchestrate-pm-alignment
skills.description: PM alignment coach and router — the entry point for all PM coaching modes

skills.name: situation-retrospective
skills.description: Map a real situation against the 7 PM excellence clusters and surface gaps

skills.name: team-perspective-reveal
skills.description: Generate what your designers and engineers would likely say about you in a peer survey

skills.name: adversarial-roleplay
skills.description: Practice difficult team dynamics via live roleplay with a frustrated designer or engineer

skills.name: decision-audit
skills.description: Structured retrospective of a specific decision — was it high quality, not just lucky?

skills.name: blind-spot-scan
skills.description: Comprehensive interview to surface systematic behavioral patterns you can't see yourself

skills.name: verbalized-sampling
skills.description: Generate genuinely diverse outputs using Verbalized Sampling (Zhang et al. 2025) — counteracts LLM mode collapse in creative and ideation tasks

skills.name: tech-sensemaking
skills.description: Analyze technology announcements for non-obvious strategic implications using Verbalized Sampling — works against any context (business, product, codebase, project, career)

skills.name: focal-point-finder
skills.description: Find, evaluate, or create focal points (Schelling points) in coordination and negotiation problems — based on Thomas Schelling's The Strategy of Conflict

skills.name: rulebook-for-arguments
skills.description: Construct, critique, and audit arguments using Weston's complete methodology — build positions, stress-test logic, identify fallacies, write argumentative essays, or steelman both sides of a debate

skills.name: vibe-code-leaf-finder
skills.description: Classify every file in a codebase as LEAF (safe to vibe code), BRANCH (AI drafts, human reviews), or TRUNK (human writes) — grounded in Erik Schluntz's leaf-vs-trunk framework from Anthropic's 'Vibe coding in prod' talk
